title: Watch Out for Your Agents! Investigating Backdoor Threats to LLM-Based Agents
contributions_summary: This paper introduces the first comprehensive investigation of backdoor attacks on LLM-based agents, revealing that their multi-step reasoning and environmental interaction enable fundamentally new and more covert attack vectors beyond traditional LLM backdoors. The authors formally define three distinct attack categories—Query-Attack (trigger in user input), Observation-Attack (trigger in environment feedback), and Thought-Attack (malicious intermediate reasoning with unchanged final output)—and demonstrate their high effectiveness through extensive experiments on AgentInstruct and ToolBench, achieving up to 80% attack success rates with minimal poisoning. Crucially, they show that existing textual backdoor defenses fail against these agent-specific threats, particularly Thought-Attack, which leaves no trace in the final output, thereby exposing a critical and previously overlooked security gap that demands new, targeted defense mechanisms for agent systems.
